Then he mentions people gathered in someone's front yard. When he approached, he saw a yard sale and bought a TV and a flashlight. So the key point here is that the narrator was at a yard sale where people were selling items. The next time, he went to another yard sale and bought a bigger TV. The text also mentions that he started sending boxes of "American junk" like TVs, toasters, etc., to his father who started a resale business. Yard sales are typically events where people sell their unwanted or used items, usually in their own yard. The passage clearly states that the narrator bought things at yard sales, implying that people sell unwanted items there. Looking at the options: A. Sell their front yards at a low price – that doesn't make sense. Yard sales aren't about selling the yard itself. B. Throw away trash in someone's yard – the text mentions garbage day and people throwing out stuff, but the yard sale is a different event where people are selling items, not just throwing away trash. C. Gather in someone's yard to enjoy things – the passage says people were gathered, but they were there to buy or sell items, not just to enjoy. The term "enjoy" is vague here. D.
